title: 十个好人Ten innocent people
description:
This game is only available in Simplified Chinese

The script contains 150,000 chinese words, featuring 28 major characters including the protagonist, with over 100 characters in total. The playtime is approximately 4.5 to 5 hours.

There are 7+1 endings: seven main endings, plus one early exit ending.

Unlike most text-based adventure games, this title features a health system and a money system.

Regarding the health system:

First, when faced with choices, making the wrong decision may result in a reduction of health points.

Second, the game includes text-based combat. Players must make correct combat decisions based on information provided by the text and the scene. Winning a battle may yield rewards (though not always); losing a battle will always result in a reduction of health points.

Third, players can use money to purchase items that restore health points. As the story progresses, some NPCs may also offer items as part of exchanges. There are other ways to restore health points as well.

Regarding the money system:

Silver is used as currency in this game. Silver can be used to cover expenses that arise during gameplay. Expenses are divided into two categories: mandatory expenses and optional expenses. If you lack sufficient silver for a mandatory expense, one health point will be deducted as an alternative.

Silver can be used to purchase items. Some of these items may help you achieve a good ending, while others may be completely useless. For example, you can use silver to buy strategy kits, but the hints inside may merely provide supplementary story information—they may not help you distinguish between good and bad NPCs, nor will they tell you how to deal with them. Of course, strategy kits may also provide useful hints, and it can be assured that they will never deliberately mislead the Emissary.

Commissioned by the Righteous Army — an assembly of righteous men and women from across the land — and acting under the decree of the "Order of Ten," the Emissary arrives in this nation: the Kingdom of Qian. Overwhelmed by excessive greed, the Kingdom of Qian has fallen into the demonic path. Its people pursue profit maximization above all else, treating friends, neighbors, and even their own flesh and blood as commodities to be sold to the highest bidder. Here, what is sold is not only freedom and dignity — the human body itself, under the name "spirit soul," has also been added to the list of goods.

The "Order of Ten" is this kingdom's last chance. The Emissary must infiltrate the nation in secret, searching for the remaining traces of justice and kindness. If he can find them, then this land will be given an opportunity to reform. If not, what awaits the Kingdom of Qian is nothing but —

annihilation.
description_short: This place has long fallen into the abyss. Yet before deciding annihilation, You, who once executed the decree, are now the chief envoy. In a country that preys on the kind-hearted, can you find ten truly good people?
